candidate_summary: NGATU-DEFECT is a per-head topological defect detector for transformer attention that treats RoPE phase as a 1D grid, computes a sliding-window cohomology cocycle invariant on phase differences, and applies a per-head correction-projection P_repair to smooth detected phase-deviation cells, optionally aggregating persistent-homology H0/H1 features across heads.

results.name: Topology-Aware Attention Persistent Homology Euler Biases (2605.03163)
results.description: Topology-aware attention injects persistent-homology H0-H2 + Euler-characteristic biases into attention logits to model structural patterns.
keyword_matches: topological defect detection RoPE phase (partial: topological)
persistent homology positional grid (partial: persistent homology)
results.rationale: Strong overlap on persistent-homology bias for attention but uses logit-bias injection rather than cocycle defect detection + correction-projection repair. The PH multi-head aggregation component of NGATU-DEFECT closely matches, but the core RoPE-phase cocycle + P_repair mechanism is absent. Same artefact family (PH applied to attention) but different effect (bias injection vs defect repair).

results.name: Surgical Repair of Collapsed Attention Heads in ALiBi Transformers (2603.09616)
results.description: Identifies attention-head collapse pathology in BLOOM/ALiBi and proposes surgical per-head repair projection.
keyword_matches: cohomology cocycle attention head (partial: attention head)
phase-deviation pattern repair projection (partial: repair projection)
topological defect detection RoPE phase (partial: defect-like collapse)
results.rationale: Direct hit on the per-head defect detection + repair-projection mechanism, though defect concept is head-collapse on BOS rather than RoPE-phase cocycle. Same artefact (per-head repair projection on detected pathological heads) and very similar effect, with different defect-detection mechanism. Functionally very close to P_repair component.

results.name: xPerT: Extended Persistence Transformer (2410.14193)
results.description: Scalable transformer on persistence diagrams; multi-resolution PH features as input for downstream tasks.
results.rationale: Uses multi-resolution PH features in transformer architecture, overlapping with the multi-head PH aggregation aspect of NGATU-DEFECT. However, processes PH diagrams as input rather than computing cocycle on RoPE phase or applying defect-repair projection. Adjacent but not a functional twin.

results.name: Persistent Homology Structural Overfitting Detection Transformer 2026
results.description: PH dissects transformer embedding topology evolution; short-lived unstable topologies signal overfitting; long-lived stable topologies signal generalization.
keyword_matches: persistent homology positional grid (partial: persistent homology)
topological defect detection RoPE phase (partial: topology + detection)
results.rationale: Uses PH for detecting transformer pathologies (overfitting as defect proxy) but the operationalization is on embedding topology evolution rather than per-head RoPE-phase cocycle. No correction-projection mechanism present. Same family of PH-based defect detection but different artefact level.

results.name: ComplexFormer Head-Specific Complex Vector Attention (2505.10222)
results.description: Per-head complex-valued attention with phase representation; per-head rotation phase as architectural primitive.
keyword_matches: cohomology cocycle attention head (partial: attention head)
topological defect detection RoPE phase (partial: phase)
results.rationale: Treats per-head rotation phase as a first-class primitive overlapping with the RoPE-phase substrate NGATU-DEFECT operates on, but does NOT compute cohomology cocycle, defect detection, or apply repair projection. Provides the underlying phase representation but not the defect-repair mechanism.

results.name: Persformer Transformer Topological ML (2112.15210)
results.description: Transformer learns on persistence-diagram input; structural defects encoded as topological invariants via attention.
keyword_matches: topological defect detection RoPE phase (partial: topological + defects)
results.rationale: Encodes structural defects as topological invariants on persistence diagrams via attention, conceptually adjacent to cocycle-invariant defect detection but operates on different substrate (PH diagrams not RoPE phase) and lacks correction-projection repair step. Same family, different mechanism.

results.name: Persistent Homology Whitens Transformer BERT Compression (2312.10702)
results.description: PH analysis of attention maps used to whiten/compress BERT; topology of attention heads informs pruning/repair.
keyword_matches: persistent homology positional grid (partial: persistent homology)
cohomology cocycle attention head (partial: attention head)
phase-deviation pattern repair projection (partial: repair via pruning)
results.rationale: PH applied to attention maps to drive head-level pruning/repair overlaps with multi-head PH aggregation for defect detection. However the mechanism is whitening/pruning rather than per-head cocycle + correction-projection; the repair is pruning not phase smoothing. Adjacent but functionally distinct.

results.name: Rethinking Attention Output Projection Hadamard Efficient Transformers (2603.08343)
results.description: Walsh-Hadamard structured output projection replacing standard projection; per-head fixed orthogonal projection for cross-head interaction.
results.rationale: Provides a per-head structured orthogonal output projection but for efficiency reasons, not defect repair. The artefact (per-head projection) overlaps loosely with P_repair but the effect is computational restructuring rather than phase-coherence restoration. Closer to efficient-transformer literature than defect repair.

decision_rationale: Six of eight results clear the hit threshold under the OR rule, primarily via keyword overlap (>=2) on persistent-homology, attention-head, and projection terms. The two closest functional twins are Topology-Aware Attention (PH biases in attention) and Surgical Repair of Collapsed Attention Heads (per-head repair projection). Together these cover both the PH multi-head aggregation component and the correction-projection repair component, leaving only the RoPE-phase cohomology cocycle invariant as the genuinely novel piece — which the candidate itself acknowledges as a recombination. With 6/8 hits and two functional twins above 0.7, the candidate fails the novelty bar.
